zh.lifeSummary: Sarah Stone是Reverend Samuel Stone和Rebecca Wright or perhaps a second wife的女兒，於1635年4月3日出生在英國賀福爾韋斯頓 武安德 亞德。她於1658年與Thomas Butler在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加康涅狄格州哈特福德哈特福德結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和9個女兒的父母。她去世於1688年8月29日在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加康涅狄格州哈特福德哈特福德享年53歲，埋葬在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加康涅狄格州哈特福德哈特福德費爾斯特 邱奇 本 克里斯蒂。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Sarah Stone是Reverend Samuel Stone和Rebecca Wright or perhaps a second wife的女儿，于1635年4月3日出生在英国賀福爾韋斯頓 武安德 亞德。她于1658年与Thomas Butler在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加康涅狄格州哈特福德哈特福德结婚。他们至少是5个儿子和9个女儿的父母。她去世于1688年8月29日在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加康涅狄格州哈特福德哈特福德享年53岁，埋葬在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加康涅狄格州哈特福德哈特福德費爾斯特 邱奇 本 克里斯蒂。

nameMeanings.name: Stone
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from Middle English ston(e) ‘stone, rock’ (Old English stān). The surname may be topographic, for someone who lived on stony ground, by a notable outcrop of rock, or by a stone boundary-marker or monument, or habitational, from a place called Stone, such as those in Buckinghamshire, Devon, Gloucestershire, Hampshire, Kent, Somerset, Staffordshire, and Worcestershire.
Irish (Kilkenny): adopted for Irish Ó Clochartaigh (see Clougherty ) and/or Ó Clochasaigh (see Clohessy ), and possibly several other names containing or thought to contain the element cloch ‘stone’.
Americanized form (translation into English) of various surnames in other languages, meaning ‘stone’, including Jewish Stein , Norwegian Steine, French Lapierre .

variants.name: Stoner
meanings: English (Sussex): topographic name from Middle English ston(e) ‘stone’ + -er, used for someone who lived by a prominent rock or stone, or perhaps who came from a place called Stone, although this formation is more rare. See Stone and compare Stoneman .
Americanized form (translation into English) of German   Steiner  .

variants.name: Stones
meanings: English (Yorkshire and Lancashire): variant of Stone with plural or post-medieval excrescent -s. A number of the places called Stone are sometimes recorded with plural spellings in the medieval period, including Stone (Staffordshire), recorded as Stanes in 1187.

variants.name: Stenner
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Hans, Kurt.

German: occupational name for a stonemason or stonecutter, or a topographic name for someone who lived on stony ground, from a derivative of Middle High German stein ‘stone’, ‘stony place’. Compare Stone and Stein .

variants.name: Clougherty
meanings: Irish: shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Clochartaigh ‘descendant of Clochartach’, a personal name, probably formed with cloch ‘stone’, Stone being an alternative Anglicized form of the surname.

variants.name: Roach
meanings: of Norman origin, from Old French and Middle English roche ‘rock, cliff, promontory’, sometimes a translation of Stone . The surname may have been topographic, denoting someone who lived on or by a prominent rock or rocky place, or a habitational name for someone who lived or came from a place so named, such as one of those called Roque or Roche(s) in Normandy and neighboring regions, or Roche in Cornwall, Roach Farm in Clyst Hydon (Devon), or Roch in Pembrokeshire (see Roch ).

in Lancashire perhaps referring to the River Roch, which runs through Rochdale; the river name is probably a back-formation from Recedham, an early name for Rochdale recorded in 1086, derived from Old English reced ‘building, house, hall’ + hām ‘village, homestead’. Alternatively, the Lancashire name could be a shortened form of the now rare or extinct surname Rochdale alias Rachedale, Rachdall, a habitational name from Rochdale (Lancashire). From the 18th century onward the name is difficult to distinguish from Roch , borne by Irish migrants to South Lancashire, especially Liverpool.

variants.name: Stoneman
meanings: English: perhaps an occupational name for someone who was the servant (Middle English man) of a person surnamed Stone . Alternatively, the name may have been an alias of Stone , used for someone who lived by a prominent stone or rock or at a minor place so named (Middle English ston + man).
English: occasionally perhaps an occupational name for someone who worked with stone or who sold precious stones; a jeweler.

English: perhaps from an unrecorded Middle English personal name Ston(e)man (Old English Stān(e)mann), a pet form of Old English personal names beginning with Stān- (from the word ‘stone’, ‘rock’) + the Old English hypocoristic suffix -mann.

nameMeanings.name: Sarah
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Biblical name, borne by the wife of Abraham and mother of Isaac. According to the Book of Genesis, she was originally called Sarai (possibly meaning ‘contentious’ in Hebrew), but had her name changed by God to the more auspicious Sarah ‘princess’ in token of a greater blessing (Genesis 17:15, ‘And God said unto Abraham, As for Sarai thy wife, thou shalt not call her name Sarai, but Sarah shall her name be’). This has been one of the most enduringly popular girls' names.

variants.name: Sara
meanings: Variant of   Sarah .  This is the form used in the Greek of the New Testament (Hebrews 11:11).

historicEvents.description: The English began to arrive in 1636

historicEvents.title: The English Civil War
historicEvents.description: A series of conflicts regarding England's governance during the years 1642 to 1651 is now known as The English Civil War. Charles I summoned supporters to join him against his enemies in Parliament. In October 1642, nearly 10,000 men fought for Charles I and chased Parliament across the River Tamar. Fighting continued for years and was finally ended at the Battle of Worcester on September 3, 1651, with a Parliamentarian victory.

historicEvents.title: Glorious Revolution
historicEvents.description: The Glorious Revolution brought the downfall of Catholic King James II and the reign of his Protestant daughter Mary and her Dutch husband, William III of Orange.

en.lifeSummary: Sarah Stone was born on 3 April 1635, in Weston under Penyard, Herefordshire, England as the daughter of Reverend Samuel Stone and Rebecca Wright or perhaps a second wife. She married Thomas Butler in 1658, in Hartford, Hartford, Connecticut Colony, British Colonial America. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 9 daughters. She died on 29 August 1688, in Hartford, Hartford, Connecticut Colony, British Colonial America, at the age of 53, and was buried in First Church of Christ, Hartford, Hartford, Connecticut Colony, British Colonial America.

fr.lifeSummary: Sarah Stone est née le 3 avril 1635, à Weston under Penyard, Herefordshire, Angleterre, fille de Reverend Samuel Stone et Rebecca Wright or perhaps a second wife. Elle a épousé Thomas Butler en 1658, à Hartford, Hartford, Connecticut Colony, Amérique coloniale britannique. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils et 9 filles. Elle est décédée le 29 août 1688, à Hartford, Hartford, Connecticut Colony, Amérique coloniale britannique, à l’âge de 53 ans, et a été enterrée à First Church of Christ, Hartford, Hartford, Connecticut Colony, Amérique coloniale britannique.

it.lifeSummary: Sarah Stone è nata il il 3 aprile 1635 nel Weston under Penyard, Herefordshire, England come figlia di Reverend Samuel Stone e Rebecca Wright or perhaps a second wife. Lei sposato Thomas Butler nel 1658, nel Hartford, Hartford, Connecticut Colony, British Colonial America. Erano genitori di almeno 5 figli e 9 figlie. Morì il 29 agosto 1688, nel Hartford, Hartford, Connecticut Colony, British Colonial America, all'età di 53 anni, e fu sepolta a First Church of Christ, Hartford, Hartford, Connecticut Colony, British Colonial America.

ja.First Church of Christ, Hartford, Hartfo: イギリス植民地アメリカコネチカット州ハートフォードハートフォードフィルスト チャーチ オフ チリスト
ja.West Hartford, Hartford, Connecticut Col: イギリス植民地アメリカコネチカット州ハートフォードウエスト ディヴィシオン パーイッシュ
ja.Hartford, Hartford, Colony of Connecticu: アメリカ合衆国コネチカット州ハートフォードハートフォード
ja.Hartford, Hartford County, Connecticut C: イギリス植民地アメリカコネチカット州ハートフォードハートフォード
ja.lifeSummary: Sarah Stoneは1635年4月3日にイギリスハーエフォードシュアイアウエストン ウンダー ペニーアルドでReverend Samuel StoneとRebecca Wright or perhaps a second wifeの娘として生まれた。1658年にイギリス植民地アメリカコネチカット州ハートフォードハートフォードでThomas Butlerと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも5人の息子と9人の娘の親であった。彼女は1688年8月29日にイギリス植民地アメリカコネチカット州ハートフォードハートフォードで53歳で死去し，イギリス植民地アメリカコネチカット州ハートフォードハートフォードフィルスト チャーチ オフ チリストにて埋葬された。

ko.Hartford, Hartford, Connecticut Colony,: 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 코네티컷 주 하트퍼드 하트퍼드
ko.Hartford, Hartford, Connecticut, British: 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 코네티컷 주 하트퍼드 하트퍼드
ko.First Church of Christ, Hartford, Hartfo: 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 코네티컷 주 하트퍼드 하트퍼드 피르스트 c후르치 프 크리스트
ko.West Hartford, Hartford, Connecticut Col: 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 코네티컷 주 하트퍼드 웨스트 디비시언 파리시
ko.Hartford, Hartford County, Connecticut C: 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 코네티컷 주 하트퍼드 하트퍼드
ko.lifeSummary: Sarah Stone(은)는 1635년 4월 3일 영국 허퍼드실르 웨스턴 운더 펜야어드에서 Reverend Samuel Stone(와)과 Rebecca Wright or perhaps a second wife의 딸로 태어났다. 그녀는 1658년 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 코네티컷 주 하트퍼드 하트퍼드에서 Thomas Butler(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 아홉 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1688년 8월 29일 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 코네티컷 주 하트퍼드 하트퍼드에서 53세의 나이로 사망했으며 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 코네티컷 주 하트퍼드 하트퍼드 피르스트 c후르치 프 크리스트에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Sarah Stone nasceu em 3 de abril de 1635, em Weston under Penyard, Herefordshire, Inglaterra filha de Reverend Samuel Stone e Rebecca Wright or perhaps a second wife. Ela casou-se com Thomas Butler em 1658, em Hartford, Hartford, Conecticute, British Colonial America. Eles tiveram pelo menos 5 filhos e 9 filhas. Ela faleceu em 29 de agosto de 1688, em Hartford, Hartford, Conecticute, British Colonial America, com 53 anos, e foi sepultada em First Church of Christ, Hartford, Hartford, Conecticute, British Colonial America.

ru.lifeSummary: Sarah Stone родилась 3 апреля 1635 г. в местности вестон ундир пенярд, хирефордшир, Англия. Её родителей звали Reverend Samuel Stone и Rebecca Wright or perhaps a second wife. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Thomas Butler в 1658 г. в местности хартфорд, хартфорд, Коннектикут, бритиш колоняль амирика. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 9 дочерей. Она умерла 29 августа 1688 г. в местности хартфорд, хартфорд, Коннектикут, бритиш колоняль амирика в возрасте 53 лет и была похоронена в местности фирст чурч оф краист, хартфорд, хартфорд, Коннектикут, бритиш колоняль амирика.

es.lifeSummary: Sarah Stone nació el 3 de abril de 1635, en Weston under Penyard, Herefordshire, Inglaterra como hija de Reverend Samuel Stone y Rebecca Wright or perhaps a second wife. Se casó con Thomas Butler en 1658, en Hartford, Hartford, Connecticut Colony, British Colonial America. Fueron padres de por lo menos 5 hijos y 9 hijas. Murió el 29 de agosto de 1688, en Hartford, Hartford, Connecticut Colony, British Colonial America, a la edad de 53 años, y fue sepultada en First Church of Christ, Hartford, Hartford, Connecticut Colony, British Colonial America.
